notes: log link both dpars (R/methods.R:5041). Fixed mu coefficients match base glm(Gamma(link="log")) to tol 1e-4 (test-comparators.R) and drive a control=drm_control(se=TRUE) emmeans reference grid (test-emmeans-methods.R), so the SE/delta-method machinery runs, not just point estimates. No gamma-specific Monte Carlo coverage study exists in-repo, so the tier stops at interval_feasible, not inference_ready_with_caveats/supported.

drm_validate_reml_spec aborts unconditionally for any model_type other than gaussian/biv_gaussian ("REML is implemented only for univariate and bivariate Gaussian models"); applies regardless of dpar, effect_type, or provider.

sigma is the coefficient of variation (shape=1/sigma^2, scale=mu*sigma^2), log link. sigma~z fixed formula verified against an independent dgamma() log-likelihood sum (test-gamma-location-scale.R:121-143) and against qgamma-generated small/large-CV edge cases. Same interval-machinery caveat as mu: SE runs by default, no dedicated coverage simulation on record.
Same unconditional non-Gaussian REML ban as the mu/fixed/REML row.

validate_positive_continuous_mu_random_terms admits only independent (uncorrelated) intercepts/slopes -- any covariance_label is rejected. Test fits (1|id) (n_id=42): pdHess=TRUE, sd estimate within 0.25 of truth, id-effect vs. true-effect correlation >0.45, and profile_targets() reports profile_ready=TRUE for sd:mu:(1|id) (profile CI mechanically available). No simulation-based coverage study for gamma REs specifically -> interval_feasible, not inference_ready/supported.
Non-Gaussian REML ban.

validate_positive_continuous_sigma_random_terms aborts unconditionally whenever any sigma-side random term is present ("Non-Gaussian sigma random effects are not implemented for {family_label}"); test asserts the exact error pattern "sigma.*random effects" for `sigma ~ 1 + (1 | id)`.
Double-blocked: sigma REs already rejected at ML; REML is also independently banned for all non-Gaussian model_types.

Independent slope-only `(0 + x | id)` implemented and tested for gamma among 6 non-Gaussian families sharing this harness: pdHess=TRUE, sd estimate >0.03, slope-effect vs. truth correlation >0.25, profile_targets() confirms a sd:mu:(0+x|id) profile target exists. Correlated mean-scale block `(1 | p | id)` is rejected (test-gamma-location-scale.R:350-357, error "random intercepts"), and correlated/multiple mu slopes `(1 + x | id)` are rejected too (same file:342-349, error "Only independent").
Non-Gaussian REML ban.
Same unconditional sigma-RE ban as the intercept case; no slope-specific carve-out exists in validate_positive_continuous_sigma_random_terms.
Double-blocked, as for ordinary_re_intercept/sigma/REML.

Covers BOTH unlabelled intercept-only `relmat(1 | id, K = K)` and unlabelled intercept+one-slope `relmat(1 + x | id, K = K)` forms -- validate_gamma_relmat_mu_structured_term (R/drmTMB.R:7831-7861) is the ONLY family-specific structured-mu carve-out for gamma. All native in-repo test fits use `control = drm_control(se = FALSE)` (SE explicitly off), so there is no interval evidence. Q-series board cell qseries_gamma_mu_relmat_rejected (fit_status=point_fit, interval_status=unsupported, denominator_policy=no_denominator_local_debug_only) and qseries_nongaussian_structured_slope_neighbors_recovery back a 90/90-converged, pdHess=TRUE, n_levels{10,20,30}x30-seed recovery ladder for the one-slope form (RMSE sd_int 0.11->0.07, RMSE sd_slp 0.07->0.03), plus a null-slope separability control and a non-identity AR(1) relatedness check. Point-fit/recovery only -- not interval, coverage, inference_ready, supported, or bridge evidence.
REML banned for all non-Gaussian model_types irrespective of structure_provider/q_gate.

Representative row for the entire beyond-q1 region. Labelled covariance `relmat(1 + x | p | id)` is rejected ("unlabelled q=1", R/drmTMB.R:7843-7849) and multiple/correlated structured slopes `relmat(1 + x + z | id)` are rejected ("intercept and one-slope", R/drmTMB.R:7850-7859); both error strings are directly tested. Stands in for q2/q4/q6/q8/q12 and all labelled or multi-slope relmat variants, which remain rejected_by_design; REML for this region is independently rejected too (R/drmTMB.R:1978-1986).

evidence_source: R/drmTMB.R:7044-7061 (drm_reject_phase1_terms); R/drmTMB.R:3999-4016 (drm_build_gamma_ls_spec extracts only relmat before the phase1 check); tests/testthat/test-julia-phylo-nongaussian.R:1-19,104-150
notes: No mu_phylo extraction exists in drm_build_gamma_ls_spec (only mu_relmat is pulled out, R/drmTMB.R:4001-4003), so `mu ~ phylo(...)` falls through to drm_reject_phase1_terms and errors "Structured-effect syntax is planned, not implemented." The separate engine="julia" DRM.jl bridge DOES fit a Gamma phylogenetic mean-intercept model (test-julia-phylo-nongaussian.R, explicitly documented there as "net-new capability, not TMB parity"); that route is outside this native model_type=5 census. Representative row also stands for all q_gates and REML (independently rejected, R/drmTMB.R:1978-1986).
Same generic phase1-reject path as phylo/spatial/phylo_interaction -- no mu_animal extraction exists for gamma in drm_build_gamma_ls_spec. Representative row for all q_gates and REML.
Same generic phase1-reject path -- no mu_spatial extraction exists for gamma. Representative row for all q_gates and REML.

Same generic phase1-reject path; no gamma-specific test exercises phylo_interaction directly (grep found none), so this row is inferred from the shared structured_marker_names() list and the absence of any gamma extraction for it, not from a direct error-string test. Representative row for all q_gates and REML.

drm_build_gamma_ls_spec never extracts a sigma-side structured marker for any provider. A `sigma ~ relmat(...)` term is left in sigma_entry$rhs and is caught by the generic drm_reject_phase1_terms check applied to the sigma formula (R/drmTMB.R:4014-4016 -> 7044-7061); ordinary sigma `(1|id)` terms are separately caught by the unconditional validate_positive_continuous_sigma_random_terms abort (R/drmTMB.R:4007-4012). Representative row for all q_gates and REML.
